prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
Ebenezer had seen a lot as the lighthouse keeper on this desolate stretch of coast, guiding ships and sailors to safety. But when the thick fog rolled in, something different stirred silently in the gloom.

It landed in the water with a gentle splash, just at the edge of visibility. He could discern a smooth, curved shape, blue and glistening - a dolphin or a shark, possibly. But then, with a swirling distortion of the sea, it rose half above the surface, and Ebenezer gasped as he saw the enormous eye staring at him. It was oval, black, and deep with intelligence.

He stumbled back into the lighthouse, shaken. He returned minutes later with a flashlight, determined to see more clearly through the fog, but the sea was impenetrable, hiding its secrets as though nothing had ever disturbed it.
